questions.q: In which year was the Guru Dutt film Pyaasa released?
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt's acclaimed film Pyaasa was released in 1957.

questions.q: Guru Dutt was active in which industry of Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt was a celebrated director, actor and producer of Hindi cinema.

questions.q: Which 1959 Guru Dutt film is widely regarded as India's first film shot in CinemaScope?
questions.explanation: Kaagaz Ke Phool (1959) was India's first feature film shot in the CinemaScope format.

questions.q: Besides directing, in which other major film role did Guru Dutt frequently appear?
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt acted as the lead in many of his own films, including Pyaasa and Kaagaz Ke Phool.

questions.q: In which year did Guru Dutt die?
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt died on 10 October 1964 at the age of 39.

questions.q: Which cinematographer was Guru Dutt's most famous long-time collaborator?
questions.explanation: V. K. Murthy was Guru Dutt's regular cinematographer, known for the lighting in Pyaasa and Kaagaz Ke Phool.

questions.q: The Guru Dutt film Pyaasa centres on the struggles of a man in which profession?
questions.explanation: Pyaasa tells the story of Vijay, a struggling and unappreciated poet.

questions.q: In which decade did Guru Dutt make his most celebrated films, Pyaasa and Kaagaz Ke Phool?
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt made his classics Pyaasa (1957) and Kaagaz Ke Phool (1959) during the 1950s.

questions.q: Guru Dutt's films are generally described as having what kind of mood and visual style?
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt is remembered for visually expressive, melancholic films exploring the artist and society.

questions.q: Which singer-actress did Guru Dutt marry?
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt married the playback singer Geeta Dutt (Geeta Roy).

questions.q: Which music director composed the songs for Guru Dutt's Pyaasa (1957)?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman composed the celebrated score and songs for Pyaasa.

questions.q: Which actress played the golden-hearted courtesan opposite Guru Dutt in Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: Waheeda Rehman played Gulabo, the courtesan who believes in Vijay's poetry, in Pyaasa.

questions.q: Which poet-lyricist wrote the famous songs, including 'Jinhe Naaz Hai Hind Par', for Guru Dutt's Pyaasa?
questions.explanation: Sahir Ludhianvi wrote the lyrics for Pyaasa, including 'Jinhe Naaz Hai Hind Par'.

questions.q: What was the central setting and theme of Guru Dutt's Kaagaz Ke Phool (1959)?
options: The decline of a film director in the movie industry
A village struggling against a landlord
A courtroom drama about a wrongful conviction
A romance set during a war
questions.explanation: Kaagaz Ke Phool depicts the rise and tragic decline of a film director, set within the film industry.

questions.q: In which city was Guru Dutt born in 1925?
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt was born in 1925 in Bangalore (Bengaluru).

questions.q: Which 1951 film, a crime thriller, marked Guru Dutt's debut as a director?
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt made his directorial debut with the 1951 thriller Baazi, produced by Dev Anand's Navketan.

questions.q: Which actor and friend gave Guru Dutt his first directing break by producing Baazi (1951)?
questions.explanation: Dev Anand, an old friend of Guru Dutt, produced Baazi under his Navketan banner, launching Dutt's directing career.

questions.q: Which 1962 film, produced by Guru Dutt and adapted from a Bengali novel, starred Meena Kumari as a lonely aristocratic wife?
questions.explanation: Sahib Bibi Aur Ghulam (1962), produced by Guru Dutt, starred Meena Kumari as Chhoti Bahu.

questions.q: Which 1960 Guru Dutt production, a Muslim social with a hit title song, was a major commercial success for him?
questions.explanation: Chaudhvin Ka Chand (1960), with its famous title song, was a big commercial hit for Guru Dutt.

questions.q: The famous beam-of-light shot, where two beams cross in a dance hall, appears in which Guru Dutt film?
questions.explanation: The celebrated beam-of-light effect, created by V. K. Murthy, features in the song 'Waqt Ne Kiya' in Kaagaz Ke Phool.

questions.q: Which song picturised on Guru Dutt closes Pyaasa as the disillusioned poet condemns a hypocritical world?
options: Aaj Sajan Mohe Ang Laga Lo
Yeh Duniya Agar Mil Bhi Jaaye
questions.explanation: 'Yeh Duniya Agar Mil Bhi Jaaye To Kya Hai' is the climactic song in which Vijay rejects a corrupt society in Pyaasa.

questions.q: What was Guru Dutt's birth name before he adopted the name by which he is known?
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt was born Vasanth Kumar Shivashankar Padukone in 1925.

questions.q: At which institution in Almora did the young Guru Dutt train, shaping his early artistic sensibility?
options: The Film and Television Institute of India
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt trained in dance and the performing arts at Uday Shankar's India Culture Centre in Almora.

questions.q: Which Pune film studio did Guru Dutt join in the mid-1940s, working early in his career before becoming a director?
questions.explanation: Guru Dutt joined the Prabhat Film Company in Pune in 1944, where he met Dev Anand and Rehman.

questions.q: How did Guru Dutt's film Kaagaz Ke Phool fare at the box office on its release in 1959?
options: It was banned by the censors
questions.explanation: Kaagaz Ke Phool was a commercial failure on release, though it was later reappraised as a classic.

questions.q: After the failure of Kaagaz Ke Phool, Guru Dutt reportedly never officially directed another film; who is credited as the director of his later production Sahib Bibi Aur Ghulam?
questions.explanation: Abrar Alvi, Guru Dutt's long-time writer, is the credited director of Sahib Bibi Aur Ghulam (1962).

questions.q: Which 1952 film directed by Guru Dutt starred Dev Anand and Geeta Bali in a tale set in a coastal fishing village?
questions.explanation: Jaal (1952), directed by Guru Dutt, starred Dev Anand and Geeta Bali and was set in a Goan fishing community.

questions.q: Which 1955 Guru Dutt romantic comedy, which he directed and starred in opposite Madhubala, satirised modern marriage?
questions.explanation: Mr. & Mrs. '55 (1955), directed by and starring Guru Dutt with Madhubala, was a satirical romantic comedy.

questions.q: In which year was Guru Dutt's directorial debut Baazi released, the film that established the 'Bombay noir' style with his stylish camerawork?
questions.explanation: Baazi was released in 1951 and helped pioneer the noir-influenced urban crime style in Hindi cinema.

questions.q: Guru Dutt's 1954 film Aar Paar, which he directed and starred in, featured music by which composer known for his peppy rhythms?
questions.explanation: O. P. Nayyar composed the lively score for Guru Dutt's Aar Paar (1954).
